title: 10 best herbal tinctures for stomach growling
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for stomach growling are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate stomach discomfort and hunger pangs. These tinctures work by regulating digestion, soothing the stomach lining, and reducing inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for stomach growling include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, Curcuma longa, Piper nigrum, Ginkgo biloba, Pimpinella anisum, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, stomach growling can lead to digestive issues, anxiety, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with stomach growling because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. This soothing action can reduce inflammation and irritation that may be contributing to stomach rumblings. Additionally, licorice tincture's natural anti-inflammatory properties may help to relax the muscles in the digestive system, calming hunger pangs and reducing discomfort associated with an empty stomach. By promoting a healthy digestive environment, herbal licorice tinctures can provide relief from annoying stomach growling and promote overall gut well-being.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to stomach growling.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tincture have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm down excessive stomach contractions and reduce discomfort associated with hunger pangs or indigestion.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as glycyrrhetic acid, found in licorice tincture have a direct impact on the digestive system by slowing down gastric emptying time, which can help to reduce stomach growling and discomfort caused by hunger.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These complex compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the stomach, thereby reducing growling and discomfort.

Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation that may contribute to stomach growling.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoflavones and flavanones): These plant-derived compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to regulate digestion, reduce stress on the stomach, and alleviate growling sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe stomach irritations and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that may aid in digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Barks: May contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially helping to calm stomach issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried root from glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer for optimal potency.
Grind the root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Combine the 50 grams of powdered root with 250 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and filter the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with stomach growling because of its natural soothing properties that calm digestive issues. The aloe vera plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that ease irritation in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and cramping associated with hunger pangs. Additionally, herbal aloe tinctures can help regulate bowel movements, preventing bloating and gas that often accompany stomach growling. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, aloe tinctures provide natural relief from annoying stomach noises, allowing for a more comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach growling.
Soothing and healing properties: Aloe's soothing and healing properties can help calm irritated mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing discomfort and stomach growling.

Digestive enzymes and mucilage: Aloe tincture contains digestive enzymes that can help break down food more efficiently, while its mucilage (a thick, protective substance) can help soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that may contribute to stomach growling.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, which can help soothe and calm an upset stomach, reducing growling sensations.
Aloin: Aloin is a glycosidic anthraquinone that helps to stimulate digestion and improve bowel movements, reducing stomach growling and discomfort caused by slow digestion.

Aloe-catechin: Aloe-catechin is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stomach growling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin and aloin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Stems: The stems contain aloin, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and ease stomach discomfort, making it a popular choice for tinctures.
Roots: The roots of Aloe vera contain aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stomach issues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200 grams of fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants on a sunny day.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Steep the dried aloe vera pieces in 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the herbal aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years and take 20-30 drops as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with stomach growling because its natural ingredients stimulate digestive enzymes, easing bloating and discomfort. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause excessive rumbling. Spasmolytic Agent: Peppermint's volatile oils, including menthol, have been shown to relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and easing discomfort that can contribute to stomach growling.

Antispasmodic Properties: The antispasmodic properties of peppermint tinctures help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract, calming down stomach contractions that can cause growling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with stomach growling by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and relaxing the muscles to ease cramps and spasms.
Menthone: Contributes to alleviating stomach growling by reducing nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic compound): Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce the sensation of stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe stomach discomfort.
Stems: Utilized for their ability to aid digestion and relieve stomach cramps, thanks to the presence of menthol and other volatile oils.
Roots: Employed for their digestive and carminative properties, which can help alleviate stomach growling and discomfort by promoting digestion and relieving gas.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers to use in the tincture.
Chop the gathered herbs into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped herbs with 750 milliliters of 25 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 21 to 28 days to allow for infusion.
Strain and bottle the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with stomach growling because its soothing properties calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause discomfort. The calming effect of chamomile also helps to ease anxiety and stress, common triggers for stomach growling. Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to ease stomach discomfort and cramps.
Stems: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with a sharp knife or pruning shears when the buds are in full bloom.
Clean the dried flowers thoroughly with a soft-bristled brush to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Combine 1 part of the dried flowers with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the extraction process to occur.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with stomach growling because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The volatile oils present in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to feelings of hunger and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's warming nature helps to stimulate digestion and increase appetite, making it a natural remedy for addressing stomach growling and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which helps to break down food quickly and efficiently, reducing symptoms of hunger pangs and stomach growling.

Antispasmodic Agent: The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with stomach growling.

remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically gingerol and shogaol, help alleviate stomach growling by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviating nausea.

Volatile oils (specifically, zingiberene and bisabolol): The volatile oils present in Zingiber officinale tinctures help soothe the digestive system by reducing muscle spasms in the stomach and intestines, which in turn reduces stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds, which are immature rhizomes, are sometimes used for tinctures due to their high content of bioactive compounds, providing relief from stomach discomfort and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the late summer or early fall season.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold water and pat them dry with a clean towel.
Cut the roots into small pieces and combine them with 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow the roots to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the cleaned curcuma longa into fine pieces and macerate them in a glass container with 500ml of high-proof vodka.
Allow the chopped curcuma longa to steep in the vodka for at least 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid from the curcuma longa and discard the solids through a cheesecloth into another glass container.
Transfer the liquid tincture to glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the plant material by air-drying for 2-3 weeks.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife.
Steep the chopped plant material in a solvent such as 95 ethanol at a ratio of 1:3 for 2-4 weeks.
Filter the tincture using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove plant residue.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for maximum potency in mid-summer mornings.

Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing for proper maceration and extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after 24 hours of settling time, and label accurately.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and measure out 200g of the herb for extraction.
Combine the measured valeriana officinalis with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally, to allow for maceration.
After the specified time, strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Label the tincture with the date and contents, and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of 40°f or below.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with stomach growling because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb's active compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can contribute to stomach growling. Additionally, maidenhair tree tinctures may help regulate appetite and digestion, reducing the frequency and intensity of stomach rumblings. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Ginkgo biloba's antispasmodic properties can help relieve stomach cramps and spasms associated with hunger pangs, reducing the sensation of stomach growling.
Digestive stimulant: The tincture has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which may help alleviate feelings of bloating, discomfort, and stomach growling.

Antioxidant: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to stomach inflammation and discomfort that can trigger growling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with stomach growling.
Barks: Containing astringent properties, barks can calm irritation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach growling symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in early morning when dew is still present, ensuring quality and potency.
Cleanly dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or desiccation methods for 7-10 days to prevent mold growth.
Grind dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or food processor, then measure 20 grams per batch.
Combine ground ginkgo biloba with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring a 1:5 leaf-to-solvent ratio for efficient extraction.
Store the mixture in a dark place and let it steep for 14-21 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate tincture formation and optimal bioavailability.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency and quality.
Chop 250 grams of the harvested leaves into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days at room temperature, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and storing the tincture.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach growling that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Herbal remedies like ginger, peppermint, and fennel have been used for centuries to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), constipation, and diarrhea. These tinctures can also help with nausea and stomach cramps associated with menstrual cramps or food poisoning.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with stomach growling is a blend of Ginger, Peppermint, and Chamomile. Ginger helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, while Peppermint relaxes the muscles and reduces inflammation. Chamomile, with its calming properties, helps to regulate digestion and alleviate anxiety-related stomach issues. Taking these tinctures together can provide quick relief from stomach growling and discomfort, promoting a sense of balance and well-being in the digestive system. This blend can be taken sublingually or added to water for consumption.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Drugs under experimental and clinical research, the ethanolic extract of Swertia chirata significantly reduced the intensity of gastric mucosal damage induced by indomethacin and necrotizing agents. The study, which used rats as subjects, found that the extract produced a significant decrease in gastric secretion in pylorus-ligated rats, suggesting its potential to alleviate stomach problems such as growling. Additionally, the extract inhibited acetylcholine-induced contraction of guinea pig ileum, indicating its anti-cholinergic activity, which may contribute to its beneficial effects on the stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with stomach growling because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. Ginger has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea, indigestion, and bloating, making it an effective remedy for stomach growling. The tincture stimulates digestive enzymes, improves gut motility, and reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, leading to a reduction in stomach growling and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach, thereby alleviating stomach growling and discomfort.
Shogaols: Shogaols have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, helping to soothe the stomach lining and reduce irritation that may contribute to stomach growling.
Volatile oils (Zingiberene, Bisabolol): These terpenes have been found to have carminative properties, which help alleviate gas and reduce stomach discomfort, thereby alleviating stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of ginger due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and digestive issues.
Roots: Ginger roots are also used to make tinctures, as they contain similar compounds to the rhyzomes and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach discomfort.

Buds: Ginger buds are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they have a milder flavor and higher concentration of antioxidants compared to the rhyzomes or roots, which can help alleviate stomach issues without causing irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh rhizomes in the morning after the dew has dried completely and cut them into small pieces.
Steam distill the rhizome pieces for 2 hours to extract the essential oils and then dry the residue.
Combine the dried residue with 2 liters of 95% ethanol and store the mixture in a glass container for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass container.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks to allow the flavors to mature and blend.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with stomach growling because it contains compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with stomach growling.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of stomach growling.

Seeds: The Seeds are also used to make tinctures, as they are believed to have carminative properties that can help relieve gas and bloating, contributing to stomach growling.
Leaves: The Leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in tinctures to help calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can alleviate stomach growling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall season.
Clean and wash the roots thoroughly then chop them into smaller pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-100 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label it properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare tinctures helps with stomach growling because of its natural carminative properties. Leaves: They are used because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help calm the digestive system and reduce stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the herb foeniculum vulgare in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested herb in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until it reaches a brittle consistency.
Chop 1 part of dried foeniculum vulgare into small pieces and combine it with 2 parts of a solvent such as ethanol.
Steep the chopped herb mixture in a glass container for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with stomach growling because it contains quinine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe digestive issues. The tincture's alkaloids can also help to regulate blood sugar levels, which may contribute to reduced hunger and stomach growling. Quinovic acid: Exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can cause stomach irritation and contributing to the sensation of growling.
Oxytropiside alkaloid: Displays antispasmodic effects, calming down the stomach muscles and reducing the contractions that lead to stomach growling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of quinine, which has been traditionally used to alleviate stomach issues.
Barks: The barks are another key component, as they contain alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe stomach discomfort.
Stems: The stems, although less commonly mentioned, also contain alkaloids that contribute to the medicinal properties of Cinchona officinalis tinctures, aiding in the relief of stomach growling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in the morning after the plant has been dewed with water for several hours.
Dry the bark in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried bark into small pieces and weigh out 250 grams for a standard tincture recipe.
Combine the chopped bark with 1250 milliliters of high proof vodka and let it steep for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture in dark glass containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum tinctures helps with stomach growling because it contains cinnamaldehyde, a natural compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. Eugenol: Acts as an antispasmodic, helping to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, thereby reducing cramps and growling.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees in the morning.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 100g of the dried bark with 750ml of 35% ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with stomach growling because its active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Tumerone: It helps with stomach growling by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation, leading to a decrease in stomach growling.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and soak them in 250ml of 35% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids by using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for 2 months.
Filter the liquid again and transfer it to a smaller bottle for storage and use as needed.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried piper nigrum berries and 50% ethanol by volume to create the base tincture.

Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow the extraction process to begin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with stomach growling because of its unique ability to improve blood flow to the digestive system. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in ginkgo biloba help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy digestion. By increasing the oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the stomach, ginkgo biloba tinctures can help to reduce hunger pangs and alleviate stomach growling, providing a sense of comfort and relief. This makes it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which in turn can alleviate stomach discomfort and reduce growling.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to regulate gut motility, which may help to alleviate stomach growling.
Terpenoids (specifically Ginkgolide B): It has a sedative effect on the gastrointestinal tract, which can help to reduce stomach contractions and alleviate growling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part for medicinal purposes, including tinctures, due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help alleviate stomach issues.

Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are sometimes used in traditional medicine to address digestive issues, including stomach growling, possibly due to their reported ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion.

Buds: Ginkgo buds are sometimes used in herbal remedies, and their extracts may be utilized in tinctures for stomach growling, possibly due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer to early fall when the plant is mature and the leaves are fully grown.
Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and preserve the herbal properties of ginkgo biloba.
Combine 1 part of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts of a neutral solvent such as vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the solvent.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

Dry the harvested pimpinella anisum flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 2 cups of the dried pimpinella anisum flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every 2-3 days for even infusion.

remedy_constituents: Menthone: Acts as a carminative agent to help alleviate stomach growling and discomfort by relaxing the smooth muscles in the digestive tract.
Menthol: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce stomach irritation and discomfort associated with growling.
Limonene: Possesses gastroprotective properties, which may help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, thereby reducing stomach growling and discomfort.

Stems: Used for their menthol content, which helps to soothe and calm digestive issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh leaves and flowers of mentha x piperita in the morning after dew has fallen.
Chop the harvested plant material finely using a sharp knife or a tool with very small blades.
Pack the chopped plant material into a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid leaving about 1 inch of space.
Pour 2 parts of a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin over the packed plant material in the jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day.
